extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked with 4km remaining on final climb to win the stage while SCHACHMANN Maximilian defended yellow by limiting BENOOT Tiesj's gap to 47 seconds.
tactical_patterns: SCULLY Tom paced peloton while teammate BETTIOL Alberto sat passive in break, revealing EF's dual-card strategy with HIGUITA Sergio.
BENOOT Tiesj's late solo chase forced HIGUITA Sergio to work for EF, inadvertently protecting SCHACHMANN's yellow jersey by controlling tempo.
ANACONA Winner led out QUINTANA Nairo before QUINTANA Dayer contributed, showing Arkéa coordinated sibling support despite Dayer lacking GC ambitions.
DE GENDT Thomas shed ALAPHILIPPE Julian before final climb despite break cooperation, suggesting he monitored power profiles to time acceleration.

rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: Chased alone for GC, finishing 47 seconds down with 12-second deficit to yellow—calculated high-risk TT specialist climbing effort nearly succeeded.
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: Defended 36-second lead by limiting BENOOT to 12-second final gap despite having no teammates, showing mature isolated defense.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Bounced back from stage 2 crash to attack at 4km, never caught—aggressive timing suggests confidence restored midweek.
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: negative: Chased for own ambitions but inadvertently helped rival SCHACHMANN by controlling BENOOT's dangerous move—tactical awareness lapse.
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: neutral: Refused to work in break while SCULLY paced behind, confirming teams accept passenger status when protecting GC card elsewhere.
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: Dropped ALAPHILIPPE before climb despite break collaboration, revealing selective workload management preserved climbing legs.
rider_signals.SWIFT Connor: positive: QUINTANA Nairo pointed to him post-stage, confirming domestique flat protection freed climber's energy—invisible work acknowledged.
rider_signals.KANGERT Tanel: neutral: Finished 8th GC for EF after protected ride, showing team prioritized dual GC threats over single leadership.
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: Top-10 GC after winning ITT, confirming TT specialists can defend losses better than pure climbers on reduced mountain stages.
